up.method: batch fermentation; glucose quantified over time by GC-TOF-MS; specific uptake rate calculated for exponential phase

up.compound: complex-medium components (amino acids, myo-inositol from beef extract)
up.method: GC-TOF-MS of supernatant over time; specific consumption rates calculated for exponential phase

sec.method: gravimetric (ethanol precipitation, dried 60C 24 h, weighed); specific xanthan production rate calculated for exponential phase
measured in supernatant profile over time (Figure 1)

growth_records.cite: Koduru, Lokanand et al. Genome-scale metabolic reconstruction and in silico analysis of the rice leaf blight pathogen, Xanthomonas oryzae. Molecular Plant Pathology (2020). DOI:10.1111/mpp.12914. [PMC7060145]
growth_records.snippet: The main batch culture was prepared in modified glucose medium comprising 5 g/L glucose, 6 g/L beef extract, 2 g/L (NH4)2SO4 2 g/L MgSO4.7H2O, and 2 g/L KH2PO4 ... maintained at 200 rpm and 28 C ... specific uptake rates of glucose and othe
growth_records.notes: Experimental batch culture of Xoo KACC10331 used to validate GEM iXOO673. Specific growth rate, specific glucose uptake rate, and specific xanthan production rate were MEASURED experimentally over the exponential phase (two intervals: 12-18 h and 18-30 h) and shown in Figure 1a-e; predicted growth rates were 'highly consistent with the experimental measurements'. The exact numeric values (growth rate in h^-1; uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h) appear ONLY in the graphical Figure 1 and are NOT present in the machine-readable XML text, so no numbers are transcribed (would require inventing). Rates left null with is_experimental=true to flag that measured values exist in the figure. Aerobic inferred from 200 rpm shaken flask culture. pH not stated. The NGAM was assumed from X. campestris pv. campestris (Jarman and Pace 1984) - a borrowed model parameter, not a measurement in this study.
